title: Large Language Models for Introductory Computer Science Education: Content Generation, Intelligent Tutoring, And Learner Modeling
data:
MFA Khan - 2026
This dissertation studies how artificial intelligence, especially large language models 
such as GPT, can help students learn introductory computer programming when the 
models are used inside a carefully designed learning system. Instead of focusing on 
AI as a standalone tool, the dissertation follows a connected story: generating 
learning resources, building a tutoring platform, running a user study, and then 
analyzing how students behave while they program.

title: Reliable Automatic Software Bug Fixing with Large Language Models: Challenges and Solutions
data:
CT Le - 2026
Software bugs impose substantial economic and operational costs on modern 
software systems, motivating sustained interest in Automated Program Repair (APR) 
as a means to reduce debugging effort and enhance software reliability. Recent 
advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have markedly improved the state of 
the art in APR, achieving strong empirical results on widely used benchmarks and 
demonstrating an unprecedented capacity to generate plausible patches
